architectural_purpose: deterministic orchestration coordination visibility without operational coordination

unsupported_reason_visibility: future runtime coordination contracts are visible but unsupported in Phase 6

blocked_reason_visibility: coordination visibility cannot coordinate runtime behavior

stale_reason_visibility: coordination context captures stale transition evidence without repair

conflicting_reason_visibility: dispatch claims conflict with explicit non-coordination boundaries

prohibited_reason_visibility: Phase 6 coordination visibility cannot dispatch orchestration behavior

diagnostics.message: Coordination identity is modeled as deterministic descriptive governance evidence.
Unsupported coordination classifications are fail-visible and not inferred into runtime coordination.
Blocked coordination states remain descriptive and cannot activate orchestration behavior.
Stale coordination context is visible without repair or correction.
Conflicting coordination claims are visible without dispatch or authorization.
Operational coordination and orchestration dispatch are explicitly prohibited.
Coordination relationships are visible as governance evidence only and cannot dispatch or route.
Coordination visibility explicitly certifies enabled_coordination_execution_count equals 0.
Coordination visibility certifies transition, policy, and operational capability counts remain 0.

explainability_summaries.summary: A coordination relationship is prohibited when it appears to enable dispatch, runtime coordination, orchestration execution, or operational flow.
Unsupported coordination states remain visible as future governance surfaces without inference or runtime coordination.
A coordination relationship is blocked when it would imply operational coordination, which Phase 6 cannot perform.
Stale coordination context is reported deterministically and cannot be repaired automatically.
Conflicting coordination claims are fail-visible because dispatch and runtime coordination remain unavailable.
Operational coordination remains unavailable because Phase 6 provides coordination visibility only.
Orchestration dispatch remains unavailable because no dispatcher or coordination engine exists.
Orchestration activation remains unavailable because coordination records are descriptive governance evidence.
Planner integration remains unavailable because coordination visibility cannot steer operational planning.
Production consumption remains unavailable because coordination evidence is replay-safe and rollback-safe descriptive output.
Governance constraints exist to keep theoretical coordination auditable without operational runtime behavior.
Runtime coordination remains prohibited because no coordination engine, dispatcher, runtime coordinator, or operational state machine exists.

explicit_limitations: v4.3 Phase 6 creates deterministic orchestration coordination governance modeling only.
v4.3 Phase 6 makes theoretical coordination relationships visible before operational coordination.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable orchestration execution.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable operational or runtime coordination.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable orchestration dispatch or activation.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable routing, traversal, scheduling, sequencing, or dependency resolution.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable state machine execution or transition execution.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable authorization or readiness approval.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable remediation, repair, inference, recommendation, ranking, scoring, selection, or optimization.
v4.3 Phase 6 does not enable planner integration, production consumption, runtime mutation, or operational mutation.
explicit_prohibitions: No orchestration coordination engine may exist.
No operational state machine may exist.
